name: Guyana

colors.symbolism: the agricultural and forested land of Guyana
the country's mineral wealth (the Golden Arrowhead)
the zeal and dynamism of nation-building
Guyana's many rivers and waters (fimbriation around the gold pile)
the endurance of the Guyanese people (fimbriation around the red pile)

description: Known as the Golden Arrowhead. A green field bearing a long golden isoceles triangle (pile) issuing from the hoist and pointing to the fly, fimbriated in white. A shorter red triangle issues from the same hoist edge over the gold, fimbriated in black, its apex stopping short of the centre.
history: Adopted on 26 May 1966, the day Guyana gained independence from the United Kingdom. The basic design — green field with gold and red piles — was created by the American vexillologist Whitney Smith in 1960 for then-British Guiana; on adoption the College of Arms in London added the white and black fimbriations. The arrowhead structure (a pile within a pile) is so distinctive that the Guyanese flag is the canonical example for this layout in vexillology.

features.symbolism: a green field with a long golden triangle (pile, fimbriated white) issuing from the hoist and overlaid by a shorter red triangle (fimbriated black); known as the Golden Arrowhead
